name: USB-C hub with HDMI + 45W PD (generic 5-in-1)
description: The baseline ADL dock: HDMI out, USB-A ports, and Power Delivery pass-through.
notes: Category verified with the reference device; any hub meeting these specs should behave the same.

name: USB-C dock with HDMI 2.0 (4K@60)
notes: Requires a phone whose DP Alt Mode carries 4 lanes.

name: 1080p HDMI monitor (any brand)
description: Full HD monitors are the sweet spot for ADL: sharp, fast, and light on the GPU.

name: 1440p HDMI monitor (any brand)
description: QHD works well on flagship phones; expect slightly higher GPU load.

name: USB 3.x portable SSD
description: External SSD mounted through the dock, visible to Android and Linux via shared storage.
notes: Use exFAT for cross-compatibility; ext4 requires extra setup on Android.

name: Bluetooth keyboard + mouse combo
description: Wireless input paired through Android; events pass into the Linux desktop.

name: Wired USB-A keyboard
description: Plugged into the dock; zero pairing, zero latency.

name: Adjustable phone stand
description: Keeps the phone visible and cool while it drives the desktop.

name: 65W USB PD charger
description: Powers the dock and keeps the phone charging under desktop load.
notes: Pick a charger rated above the dock's pass-through so the hub's own draw is covered.
